title: Ensure app prompts for camera access on load

requirements.text: Update FaceScanComponent so that it calls `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: true })` immediately on component mount (inside a useEffect with an empty dependency array), triggering the browser's native camera permission prompt as soon as the face scan section is rendered — without requiring any user interaction such as clicking a button first. If permission is granted, the live video feed must start automatically. If permission is denied, display a clear error message instructing the user to allow camera access in their browser settings.

quotes: make app ask for camera access

acceptanceCriteria: Opening the app and navigating to the face scan section immediately triggers the browser camera permission dialog.
If camera permission is already granted, the live video feed starts automatically without any button press.
If camera permission is denied or unavailable, a visible error message is shown explaining how to enable camera access.
No additional button press or navigation step is required before the camera permission prompt appears.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/ (immutable paths).
Do not modify frontend/src/components/ui or frontend/src/main.tsx.
nonGoals: Actual ML-based face recognition or biometric template processing.
Backend changes — this is a frontend-only camera access fix.
Changing the overall routing or page layout beyond what is needed for camera auto-prompt.
